original_text: R&D investment intensity remains elevated at 19.70% of revenue in Q2 2025 (K005), with R&D expenses of $610,000,000 (C001). This represents an increase from Q1 2025's $568,000,000 (C014), Q3 2024's $524,000,000 (C021), and Q2 2024's $519,000,000 (C028), indicating continued commitment to product development and platform enhancement. The R&D intensity of nearly 20% is substantial for a mature travel platform, suggesting Airbnb is prioritizing innovation and technology infrastructure to maintain competitive positioning. This investment level may pressure near-term margins but could support long-term differentiation.
revised_text: R&D investment intensity remains elevated at 19.70% of revenue in Q2 2025 (K005), with R&D expenses of $610,000,000 (C001). This represents an increase from Q1 2025's $568,000,000 (C014), Q3 2024's $524,000,000 (C021), and Q2 2024's $519,000,000 (C028). R&D expenses have increased sequentially from Q1 2025 and year-over-year from Q2 2024.
changes_summary: Retained original chunk IDs (C001, C014, C021, C028) as they accurately support the R&D expense claims in the provided evidence, whereas the checker-suggested IDs (C003, C016, C023, C030) correspond to Operating Income, Revenue, and Assets respectively. Removed unsupported analytical claims regarding 'mature travel platform', 'innovation prioritization', 'competitive positioning', and margin/differentiation impacts per Rule 7, as these are not substantiated by the available evidence chunks. Added a factual summary of the sequential and YoY increase supported by the cited data.
